meaning: God acts as the one who declares righteous those who have faith in Jesus, combining justice with justification.
evidence_quote: just and the justifier of the one who has faith in Jesus

meaning: A person is declared righteous before God through faith alone, not through performing works of the law.
evidence_quote: one is justified by faith apart from works of the law

meaning: Justification is reckoned or counted to a person through faith, not through law-works.
evidence_quote: we hold that one is justified by faith apart from works of the law

meaning: God declares both circumcised and uncircumcised righteous through faith, acting as sole justifier of all.
evidence_quote: who will justify the circumcised by faith and the uncircumcised through faith

meaning: God being justified means his words are vindicated and shown true against all human falsehood.
evidence_quote: That you may be justified in your words

meaning: God's righteousness is a standard that human unrighteousness paradoxically highlights by contrast.
evidence_quote: our unrighteousness serves to show the righteousness of God

meaning: Condemnation of those who advocate doing evil for good ends is described as just and deserved.
evidence_quote: Their condemnation is just

meaning: Righteousness was credited (counted) to Abraham before circumcision, not after — timing of the reckoning is the focus.
evidence_quote: How then was it counted to him? Was it before or after he had been circumcised?

meaning: Righteousness here is an inner standing before God received through faith, not through the external act of circumcision.
evidence_quote: a seal of the righteousness that he had by faith while still uncircumcised

meaning: Righteousness is reckoned or credited to believers as an external accounting, not an inherent quality they possess.
evidence_quote: righteousness would be counted to them as well

meaning: Righteousness here is the inner disposition of faith, the channel through which the promise came, not legal observance.
evidence_quote: through the righteousness of faith

meaning: Being justified by works would give grounds for human boasting, but such justification does not stand before God.
evidence_quote: if Abraham was justified by works, he has something to boast about, but not before God

meaning: Faith, not deeds, is the basis on which a person is credited with righteousness — right standing before God.
evidence_quote: his faith was counted to him as righteousness

meaning: Faith is credited or reckoned to a person as righteousness — an act of accounting or formal recognition.
evidence_quote: his faith was counted to him as righteousness

meaning: Faith in the resurrected Jesus is counted (credited) to believers, mirroring how righteousness was reckoned to Abraham.
evidence_quote: It will be counted to us who believe in him who raised

meaning: Christ's resurrection is the basis by which believers receive justification — being declared righteous before God.
evidence_quote: raised for our justification

meaning: Abraham's faith was credited to him as righteousness — belief itself placed him in right standing.
evidence_quote: it was counted to him as righteousness

meaning: Abraham's faith was reckoned or credited to him as righteousness — an accounting act, not a moral achievement.
evidence_quote: it was counted to him as righteousness

meaning: Wages earned by work are reckoned as owed, not as a freely given gift.
evidence_quote: his wages are not counted as a gift but as his due

meaning: Faith, not works, is counted as righteousness — the state of right standing with God granted to the ungodly believer.
evidence_quote: his faith is counted as righteousness

meaning: God declares the ungodly person righteous, not on the basis of works but on the basis of faith.
evidence_quote: him who justifies the ungodly, his faith is counted as righteousness

meaning: Faith is credited as righteousness to the one who believes rather than works.
evidence_quote: his faith is counted as righteousness

meaning: Righteousness is something God counts or credits to a person, granted apart from any works they perform.
evidence_quote: God counts righteousness apart from works

meaning: God credits righteousness to a person as an inner standing, independently of any works they perform.
evidence_quote: God counts righteousness apart from works

meaning: The Lord's choice not to reckon sin against a person is the basis of that person's blessedness.
evidence_quote: the Lord will not count his sin

meaning: Faith being credited as righteousness means inner trust is counted as the state of right standing before God.
evidence_quote: faith was counted to Abraham as righteousness

meaning: Faith was reckoned or credited to Abraham as righteousness — an act of divine accounting, not earned status.
evidence_quote: faith was counted to Abraham as righteousness

meaning: Being justified by faith establishes a proper relationship with God, evidenced by resulting peace with him.
evidence_quote: since we have been justified by faith, we have peace with God

meaning: Justification (dikaiōma) is the outcome of the free gift, reversing condemnation that came from one trespass.
evidence_quote: the free gift following many trespasses brought justification

meaning: Righteousness is a free gift received by believers, enabling them to reign in life through Christ.
evidence_quote: the free gift of righteousness reign in life through the one man Jesus Christ

meaning: A single righteous act produces justification and life as its outcome for all people.
evidence_quote: one act of righteousness leads to justification and life for all men

meaning: One act of righteousness produces justification and life for all men, contrasting with condemnation from one trespass.
evidence_quote: one act of righteousness leads to justification and life for all men

meaning: Being made righteous is a condition conferred on many through one man's obedience, not through personal moral achievement.
evidence_quote: by the one man's obedience the many will be made righteous

meaning: Righteousness is the channel through which grace reigns, leading toward eternal life.
evidence_quote: grace also might reign through righteousness leading to eternal life

meaning: A righteous person is someone people are unlikely to sacrifice their life for, contrasted with a 'good' person who inspires more devotion.
evidence_quote: one will scarcely die for a righteous person

meaning: Being justified means having been placed in a right relationship with God, here accomplished through Christ's blood.
evidence_quote: we have now been justified by his blood

meaning: Believers are to actively reckon or regard themselves as dead to sin and alive to God.
evidence_quote: must consider yourselves dead to sin and alive to God

meaning: Righteousness is what members (body parts) serve when yielded to God rather than to sin.
evidence_quote: present your members to God as instruments for righteousness

meaning: Righteousness is the outcome produced in a person who chooses obedience rather than sin.
evidence_quote: obedience, which leads to righteousness

meaning: Righteousness is presented as a master one becomes enslaved to upon being freed from sin.
evidence_quote: having been set free from sin, have become slaves of righteousness

meaning: Righteousness is an inner orientation that, when one's members are surrendered to it, drives progress toward sanctification.
evidence_quote: present your members as slaves to righteousness leading to sanctification

meaning: Slaves of sin had no allegiance to righteousness — it had no hold or claim over them.
evidence_quote: you were free in regard to righteousness

meaning: Death frees a person from sin's hold — used here in a legal/relational sense of being released, not inner moral transformation.
evidence_quote: one who has died has been set free from sin

meaning: The commandment is described as righteous, meaning it conforms to proper standards of right conduct.
evidence_quote: the commandment is holy and righteous and good

meaning: Righteousness is the basis or cause by which the Spirit gives life within the believer.
evidence_quote: the Spirit is life because of righteousness

meaning: The speaker makes a deliberate mental reckoning, weighing present sufferings against future glory and concluding glory outweighs them.
evidence_quote: I consider that the sufferings of this present time are not worth comparing

meaning: God condemned sin in the flesh through Christ, an act of divine judgment, not an inner-being characteristic.
evidence_quote: he condemned sin in the flesh

meaning: God declares called persons righteous, placing them in right standing as a definite act in a divine sequence.
evidence_quote: those whom he called he also justified

meaning: God acts as the one who declares the elect righteous, making charges against them futile.
evidence_quote: It is God who justifies

meaning: The verse asks rhetorically who can condemn, implying no condemnation is possible given Christ's death and intercession.
evidence_quote: Who is to condemn? Christ Jesus is the one who died

meaning: The righteous requirement of the law is an inner standard fulfilled in those who walk by the Spirit, not the flesh.
evidence_quote: the righteous requirement of the law might be fulfilled in us

meaning: Righteousness is attained not by pursuit but by faith; Gentiles received it without seeking it.
evidence_quote: a righteousness that is by faith

meaning: Righteousness is presented as a goal Israel pursued through law but failed to attain.
evidence_quote: pursued a law that would lead to righteousness did not succeed

meaning: The term 'uprightness' here functions as an adverb ('rightly'), indicating that loving you is fitting or proper, not describing an inner character trait.
evidence_quote: rightly do they love you

meaning: Being upright is listed as a required inner character trait for a church leader.
evidence_quote: upright, holy, and disciplined

meaning: The person's own inner state condemns them — their conscience or actions render an internal verdict against themselves.
evidence_quote: he is self-condemned

meaning: Righteousness here refers to works humans do in conformity with right standards — but salvation does not rest on these.
evidence_quote: not because of works done by us in righteousness

meaning: Being justified by grace establishes a right standing before God, enabling inheritance of eternal life through hope.
evidence_quote: being justified by his grace we might become heirs

meaning: God's relationship with his restored people is characterised by righteousness alongside faithfulness.
evidence_quote: I will be their God, in faithfulness and in righteousness

meaning: The coming king is described as righteous, a core inner character quality paired with humility and salvation.
evidence_quote: righteous and having salvation is he, humble

meaning: Righteousness is something actively pursued as a quality the humble must seek to possess inwardly.
evidence_quote: seek righteousness; seek humility

meaning: The Lord's inner character is righteous, expressed by doing no injustice and consistently showing forth justice every morning.
evidence_quote: The Lord within her is righteous; he does no injustice

meaning: Uprightness is blocked from entering — it cannot function or find place in the public sphere.
evidence_quote: uprightness cannot enter
